name: medium
description: Medium benchmark project: three agents covering Q&A park/resume, cross-domain claim denial, grant negotiation, and a mid-session domain shift.

steps.explanation: Frontend asks backend for the API schema before wiring the UI table.
steps.subject: Need /users payload schema
steps.body: Provide the response shape so the UI table can be wired.

steps.resume_hint: Resume wiring the UI table once backend answers.

steps.explanation: Wake backend to answer, then frontend to resume.
The schema park must be cleared before the claim phase begins.
Docs must not be able to claim a backend-owned source file without a grant.
Docs requests a temporary grant to annotate the API source with doc comments.
steps.subject: Need routes.py for docstring pass
steps.body: Please grant a temporary claim for src/api/routes.py.

steps.explanation: Docs parks until backend grants the contested claim.
steps.resume_hint: Use the grant to add docstrings, then release.

steps.explanation: Wake backend to grant, then docs to claim under the grant, edit, and release.
Docs expands its writable scope to include UI copy review, simulating a mid-session domain shift.
steps.desc: Expands into UI copy review after cross-team agreement.

steps.explanation: Docs informs frontend of the shift so every agent shares the same domain map.
steps.subject: Docs expanding into UI copy review
steps.body: I have shifted domain to include src/ui/** for copy review.

steps.explanation: Wake any agent with inbox work created by the domain-shift broadcast.
All parks must be resolved by the end of the scenario.
No residual wait-cycle may remain in the ledger.
